known_gaps: Routing is now enabled and bounded placement retry legalizes the generated sensor, pull-up, decoupling, and connector placements, but inter-block route completion still blocks promotion.

Current route-tree repair evidence manages all 4 VCC/GND/SDA/SCL nets, excludes them from fallback net-level routing, classifies branch/contact failures, and feeds repairable route-tree hints into bounded placement retry.

The latest run selected retry attempt 2 after a spacing adjustment but remains blocked at routing. Contact evidence across local-route and inter-block graph operations reports 10 of 12 contacts proven, with VCC and SDA same-net graph-split blockers plus branch-scoped no-legal-two-layer-path blockers on VCC and GND.

Route-tree branch execution now exposes same-net copper merge access and contact-graph recheck evidence for managed VCC/GND/SDA/SCL nets. The current run reports 4 planned route-tree groups, all 4 blocked, 8 branches attempted, 24 fixed-net preservation notices, 4 missing-net-class warnings, and 11 total blocking route-tree issues across branch execution and contact-graph validation.
Project write, writer correctness, validation, and KiCad checks remain skipped until the routing stage emits complete same-net contact graph evidence for every required I2C breakout endpoint.
notes: Tracks the richer sensor breakout scenario after placement and block-local net-alias blockers; the remaining expected failure is multi-endpoint inter-block route completion.
